evals.name: check-before-naming
evals.prompt: I want to add a new class called LLMLogger to the reMind project that will handle logging of all LLM API calls. Before I start, check if there's anything with a similar name already in the codebase. The project is at g:/python_projects/reMind
evals.expected_output: Should find the existing LLMLogger class in backend/app/llm_logger.py and warn about the naming conflict, suggesting an alternative name

assertions.name: found-existing-llmlogger
assertions.check: The output mentions that LLMLogger already exists in the codebase

assertions.name: identified-file-location
assertions.check: The output identifies llm_logger.py as the file containing the existing LLMLogger

assertions.name: suggested-alternative
assertions.check: The output suggests an alternative name or warns about the conflict

assertions.name: token-efficiency
assertions.check: Used glossary search or similar lightweight approach instead of reading multiple source files

evals.name: session-start-orientation
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new session working on the reMind project at g:/python_projects/reMind. I need to work on the backend API routes. Give me an overview of the backend structure — what are the main classes, routers, and services?
evals.expected_output: Should provide a structured overview of backend symbols organized by module, showing routers, services, models, and key functions without reading every source file

assertions.name: covered-routers
assertions.check: The output lists the router modules (auth, ai, billing, maps, etc.)

assertions.name: covered-models
assertions.check: The output mentions key model classes (User, MindMap, Node, etc.)

assertions.name: covered-services
assertions.check: The output mentions service modules or classes

assertions.name: structured-output
assertions.check: Output is organized by module/area, not a random dump

evals.name: find-related-symbols
evals.prompt: I need to add a new authentication middleware to the reMind project at g:/python_projects/reMind. Before writing it, show me all existing auth-related functions and classes so I understand what's already there and can integrate properly.
evals.expected_output: Should find auth-related symbols across multiple files (auth.py, mcp_auth.py, oauth.py, etc.) and present them in a useful way

assertions.name: found-auth-module
assertions.check: The output covers symbols from backend/app/auth.py

assertions.name: found-mcp-auth
assertions.check: The output covers symbols from mcp_auth.py or mentions McpBearerAuthMiddleware

assertions.name: found-auth-functions
assertions.check: The output mentions key auth functions like get_current_user, hash_password, or verify_password

assertions.name: comprehensive-coverage
assertions.check: Found auth symbols across multiple files, not just one
